meta.notes: Standing orders specific to Full Council meetings. Cabinet/committee procedures differ.

public_question_time.notice_required: 12 noon, 10 clear working days before meeting
public_question_time.notes: Hard 30-minute cap. Questions not reached get written responses.

member_question_time.notice_required: 12 noon 6 weeks before to 12 noon 7 clear working days before

urgent_questions.requirements: Circumstances arose after deadline, genuine urgency, Chair and Monitoring Officer satisfaction

slot_transfer.rule: If a group submits no question, their slot transfers to another group's second question on first-come-first-served basis

member_question_time.notes: Must ask question as on agenda without introductory or explanatory speech (SO 30.11). If absent, same-group member may substitute (SO 30.10).

motion_debate.notes: All timings at Chair's discretion (SO 39). Budget speeches by Finance Cabinet Member and opposition group budget spokespersons have no time limit.

submission.notice_deadline: 12 noon 7 clear working days before meeting

slot_transfer.rule: If ruling group submits fewer than 2, available slots transfer to opposition on first-come-first-served basis

requirements.must_be: Concise, clear, to the point, maximum 250 words
requirements.notes: Must require the Council to take action or adopt a position. Motions expressing mere opinion without requiring action can be rejected.

grounds: Rescind any Full Council resolution within preceding 12 months
Substantially same as motion voted on but not carried within preceding 6 months
Require breach of Constitution or legislation
Vague, ambiguous, irrelevant, defamatory, frivolous, offensive, vexatious, unlawful, improper, or factually incorrect
Express mere opinion without requiring action
Withdrawn or not raised within preceding 6 months
Name specific service users or staff
Express support/objection during Council consultation processes

motions_that_fall.rule: Any motion not disposed of at Full Council falls. Does not carry forward to next meeting.
motions_that_fall.tactical_note: If the 120-minute total expires, remaining motions die. This is a procedural tool for time management.

general.submission: Written, handed to Chief Executive immediately after mover's speech
general.rejection: Chair, consulting Chief Executive, may reject on SO 34 grounds

friendly.rule: Motion mover may amend their own motion or accept an amendment without debate or vote
friendly.tactical_note: Bypasses debate entirely. If the mover accepts a compromise, the amended motion becomes substantive immediately.

becomes_substantive.rule: If amendment carried, it replaces the original motion. Chief Executive reads out amended motion before further amendments accepted.

subsequent.rule: If first amendment lost, further amendments may be moved to original. After all amendments considered, further debate opportunity before final vote.

exceptions: Speaking once on another member's amendment
Speaking on main issue if first speech addressed another's amendment only
Point of order (must cite specific SO)
speaking.notes: Every motion must be moved and seconded before debate. Remarks must be strictly confined to the motion under discussion.

right_of_reply.original_mover: Has right of reply at end of debate, immediately before vote
right_of_reply.on_amendment: Original motion mover has reply at close of amendment debate but may not otherwise speak on amendment
right_of_reply.tactical_note: The original motion mover always gets the last word. Amendment movers do not.

closure_motions.who_can_move: Only someone who has NOT spoken, moved, or seconded in the debate

options.motion: That the question be now put
options.effect: Forces immediate vote. If Chair deems sufficient discussion and motion carried, original mover gets reply then substantive vote taken
options.tactical_note: The guillotine. Forces an end to debate.

options.motion: That the Full Council proceed to the next business
options.effect: Kills current motion without voting on substance. Mover gets reply, then closure voted on
options.tactical_note: Effectively kills the motion without putting councillors on record on the substantive issue.
Nuclear option. Kills all remaining business.

voting.method: Show of hands, electronic means, or affirmation (if no dissent)
voting.majority: Simple majority of those present and voting
voting.seated_requirement: Must be in designated seat for vote to count (Full Council only)
voting.individual_record: Any councillor may request their vote be recorded in minutes

recorded_vote.records: Names for, against, and abstaining recorded in minutes
recorded_vote.tactical_note: Reform UK (53 seats) can always force a recorded vote. Useful for putting opposition on record.

voting.budget_votes: Mandatory recorded vote on budget/precept decisions (2014 Regulations)

casting_vote.rules: No restrictions on how used. If Chair abstains on casting vote, matter falls.

interpretation.rule: Chair's ruling on Standing Orders is final. Can be reconsidered once (with Monitoring Officer). Cannot be challenged further except by court order.

silence.rule: If Chair stands, all councillors must immediately resume seats and be silent.

powers: Move that councillor be not heard further (voted without discussion)
If continued, move that councillor leave meeting (voted without discussion)

vary_order.rule: Chair may vary agenda order at discretion, or by resolution moved and voted without debate

time_discretion.rule: All speech timings at Chair's discretion. Decision is final.

suspension.requirement: Motion moved and seconded, at least half of whole number of members present and voting
suspension.tactical_note: Reform UK with 53 of 84 seats can suspend any standing order except voting rules. This includes time limits, speaking rules, and motion caps.

allowed: Referring recommendations back to originating body
Standing Order suspension or voting matters
motions_without_notice.tactical_note: Referring recommendations back can be moved without notice to delay decisions. Business precedence changes can reorder the agenda.

majority_tools: Can suspend any non-voting standing order with half the members (SO 25)
Closure motion kills debate (SO 45) — must be moved by non-speaker
Motions that exceed 120 minutes total simply fall (SO 36)
Chair can reject amendments on SO 34 grounds
opposition_tools: Supplementary questions at Member Question Time (SO 30.14)
Amendments to motions (1 per member per motion, SO 40)
Points of order citing specific SOs (SO 38)
Request individual vote recorded in minutes (SO 46)
